purpose: Measure whether explicit channel relations improve dialogue at fixed architecture and training budget.

split_policy.unit: whole source channel, play, poem, or consented chat channel
forbidden: random token split across the same channel
selecting a checkpoint on the hidden comparison panel

candidates.representation: normalized literary bytes without channel controls
speaker-tagged turns with explicit reply edges
full channel records with vibe, lossy-memory targets, turns, and reply edges

requirements: beats A-flat by at least 10 percentage points on reply-edge plus speaker-role win rate averaged over seeds
wins at least 60 percent of a blinded 200-prompt human comparison against A-flat
does not regress author-balanced literary validation NLL by more than 2 percent
reports every seed and every task family
passes deterministic artifact and benchmark hash checks

required_artifacts: training configuration and corpus manifest per candidate and seed
promotion verdict with failed requirements retained
